title: rubus semicalvus
intro: Rubus semicalvus, also known as the half-green bramble, is a species of flowering plant in the rose family that is native to Europe. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Rubus semicalvus include potential anti-cancer and cardiovascular protective effects, as well as its ability to aid in digestion and alleviate respiratory issues. The plant is typically prepared as a tea, decoction, or tincture, and can also be used in salads or as a jam ingredient.
